title: Origin of 24 PASS: general theorem — H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF≡0 (mod 24) for all primitive Pythagorean directions
_fixture_purpose: General theorem fixture. For ALL primitive Pythagorean directions (d>e>0, gcd(d,e)=1, d+e odd), H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF≡0 (mod 24). The fundamental direction (d,e)=(2,1) achieves the minimum value 24. Machine-verified for all d≤20.

general_theorem.statement: For all d>e>0 with gcd(d,e)=1 and d+e odd: H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF and 24 | (H²-G²)
general_theorem.proof_sketch: H²-G² = (C+F)²-G² = C²+2CF+F²-G². Since C²+F²=G² (Pythagorean identity for the QA direction), this simplifies to 2CF. Similarly G²-I²=(d²+e²)²-(C-F)²=G²-C²+2CF-F²=2CF. So H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=2(2de)(d²-e²)=4de(d-e)(d+e). Divisibility by 24: (a) div by 8 — since d+e is odd, exactly one of d,e is even, so de is even; then 4de(...)=8·(something integer). (b) div by 3 — if neither d nor e is divisible by 3, then d≡1,e≡2 or d≡2,e≡1 (mod 3), giving d+e≡0 (mod 3). So 3 always divides at least one of d,e,d+e,d-e. Together: 24 | 2CF.

corollary_second_order_quadruple.statement: (I², 2CF, G², H²) is a QA quadruple: I²+(2CF)=G² and I²+2(2CF)=H²
corollary_second_order_quadruple.proof: I²+2CF = (C-F)²+2CF = C²-2CF+F²+2CF = C²+F² = G². Then G²+2CF = G²+(H²-G²)/1... = G²+2CF = I²+4CF = (C+F)²=H².
corollary_second_order_quadruple._note: The quantity 2CF=H²-G²=G²-I² is the 'e' step-element of a second-order QA quadruple built from squares of primary elements. The (b,e,d,a) structure is self-similar across the squaring map.

connection_to_mod24_arithmetic.statement: The QA cosmos orbit period π(9)=24 equals the Pisano period of Fibonacci mod 9. The same 24 appears as the exact minimum gap H²-G²=24 at the fundamental Pythagorean direction (d,e)=(2,1). This is not a coincidence: both arise from the Fibonacci/golden-ratio structure of Z[φ] and its reduction mod 9.
connection_to_mod24_arithmetic.significance: Ben Iverson identified this as the origin of QA's mod-24 structure. The number 24 is not arbitrary — it is the natural periodicity forced by the fundamental Pythagorean geometry. The formula H²-G²=2CF links QA's two key elements: C (green quadrance) and F (red quadrance).

validation_checks.description: at least 3 witnesses with correct H2_G2 values
